news.summary: The central bank is waiting for more evidence that inflation is decreasing before considering another interest rate cut.

news.headline: Global head-mounted display market to reach $26 billion by 2030, growing at CAGR of 39.2%. Meta, Sony, PICO Immersive among dominating companies.
news.summary: Dublin - A recent report on the Head Mounted Display Market has been included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. The global market for head-mounted displays was valued at US$3.3 billion in 2023 and is expected to exhibit a significant compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 39.2% over the forecast period, reaching a market size of US$26.0 billion by 2030. The growth of the Head-Mounted Display (HMD) market is being fueled by technological advancements, particularly in the field of virtual reality (VR).

Siebel, is set to engage in a fireside chat at The Citizens JMP Technology Conference on March 3 at 3:00 pm ET. The event will be accessible for replay on C3 AI's Investor Relations website (ir.c3.ai). C3 AI focuses on Enterprise AI applications.

news.headline: Financial protection uncertain as CFPB halts; Rules on fees, debt & apps on hold. Check statements regularly.
news.summary: Regulations regarding bank and credit card fees, medical debt, and payment apps are currently uncertain. One expert advises individuals to diligently review their financial statements to stay informed.

news.headline: SkyDrive partners with AeroGulf, receiving pre-order for 50 eVTOL aircraft to target air mobility opportunities in Dubai.
news.summary: SkyDrive Inc., a prominent Japanese eVTOL aircraft manufacturer, has signed a Letter of Intent with AeroGulf Services Company LLC, a Dubai-based helicopter charter company, for the potential purchase of up to 50 "SKYDRIVE" eVTOL aircraft. The partnership aims to investigate innovative air mobility prospects at Palm Jumeirah in Dubai. SkyDrive, known for its cutting-edge aircraft technology, is set to collaborate with AeroGulf Services to tap into the lucrative market opportunities in the iconic tourist spot.

news.headline: ventureLAB's Hardware Catalyst Initiative hits 5 years, with $5 million investment leading to growth in Canada's HardTech sector.
news.summary: In Markham, Ontario, ventureLAB is marking a milestone with the Hardware Catalyst Initiative, the first and only hardware and semiconductor startup incubator in Canada, which has been running for five years. Established in 2019 with an exceptional $5 million funding from FedDev Ontario, this initiative has revolutionized the Canadian HardTech industry. It has enhanced supply chains, encouraged innovative developments, and boosted startups in various sectors including MedTech, AI, automotive technology, and more.

news.headline: Semtech to discuss key topics in fireside chat at con, incl recent industry trends & financial performance, anticipate 500 participants.
news.summary: Recently, a new report revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in the past five years. The report indicated that there were 200,000 new jobs created in the last quarter, contributing to this decrease in unemployment. Additionally, the report highlighted that the economy grew by 3.2% in the same period, exceeding expectations. This positive trend is seen as a result of various government initiatives aimed at boosting job creation and economic growth. Experts believe that if this momentum continues, the country is on track to further reduce unemployment and sustain economic progress in the coming years.

news.headline: Financial 15 Split Corp. extends termination date from Dec 1, 2025 to Dec 1, 2030 - 5-year extension.
news.summary: Toronto, February 28, 2025: Financial 15 Split Corp. has recently revealed its decision to prolong the termination date of the company by an additional five years. The extension moves the initial deadline of December 1, 2025, to December 1, 2030.

news.headline: California Water Service Group appoints Kris A. Hamner as Chief HR Officer, succeeding Ronald D. Webb after a decade.
news.summary: On February 28, 2025, in SAN JOSE, California, the California Water Service Group revealed that Kris A. Hamner will become the new Vice President and Chief Human Resource Officer of the Company. This transition on the NYSE-listed Company (NYSE: CWT) signifies the retirement of Ronald D. Webb after over a decade of dedicated service. Kris A. Hamner's appointment is effective immediately and marks a significant change in the company's leadership structure.

news.headline: Callan JMB offers new lease program for vaccine management for govt and private orgs, easing economic burden.
news.summary: In Spring Branch, Texas, on February 28, 2025, Callan JMB INC. (NASDAQ: CJMB) unveiled a new initiative aimed at facilitating vaccine management for healthcare and emergency management sectors. The program offers a comprehensive lease model allowing government entities and private firms to access vital immunization services without the financial strain of upfront equipment purchases. Callan JMB specializes in integrative logistics solutions, providing storage, monitoring, and cold chain logistics. This program is designed to enhance efficiency and accessibility, ensuring crucial vaccines reach the intended recipients effectively.

news.headline: Stifel donates to Mikaela Shiffrin's "MIK100" initiative after her historic 100th win, aiming to raise $100,000 for charity.
news.summary: On February 28, 2025, Stifel (NYSE: SF), the official team naming partner of the Stifel U.S. Alpine Ski Team, commemorated Mikaela Shiffrin's monumental achievement of securing her 100th career World Cup triumph. The accomplished skier clinched the top spot in the slalom event held in Sestriere, Italy. Stifel contributed to this success by aiding Shiffrin in raising an impressive $100,000 for the Share Winter Foundation.

news.headline: DFDS awards long-term incentives to tie management pay to company's performance, aiming to retain management.
news.summary: DFDS has introduced a new long-term incentive plan for its management team to align their compensation with the company's ongoing performance, as indicated by the share price. This initiative also serves to encourage the retention of key management personnel within the organization.

news.headline: February saw a drop in 6 SEC cases and cooling of memecoin craze, among other trends.
news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.3%, its lowest level in the past five years. This decrease represents a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.2%. The report also shows that the economy added 250,000 new jobs, surpassing expectations and indicating strong growth in the labor market. Analysts attribute this positive trend to increased consumer spending and business investments. Additionally, the report highlights a notable increase in wages, with average earnings rising by 3.5% compared to last year. Overall, these findings suggest a thriving economy with robust job creation and improved financial outlook for workers.

news.headline: Shari Redstone of Paramount aims to settle President Trump lawsuit before Skydance merger.
news.summary: Redstone, the individual in charge of Paramount Studios, is currently in the process of finalizing a merger with the Hollywood studio Skydance. However, complications have arisen due to a lawsuit filed by President Trump against CBS News. The merger between Redstone's Paramount and Skydance is at risk of being affected by this legal dispute.

The Board of Directors approved the issuance of Warrants, allowing holders to purchase shares of the company's common stock. This move aims to benefit shareholders efficiently and boost shareholder value. M-tron Industries, Inc. (NYSE American: MPTI) made this announcement in Orlando, Florida.

news.headline: Mogo extends credit facility with Fortress Investment Group by three years, reducing interest rate by 100 basis points.
news.summary: A digital wealth and payments business, Mogo Inc. (NASDAQ: MOGO) (TSX: MOGO) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, informed about an amendment to its current senior credit facility with funds managed by Fortress Investment Group LLC. The amendment extends the maturity date of the facility by three years, now set at January 2, 2029. It also cuts the interest rate by 100 basis points. The credit facility is crucial in supporting Mogo's digital credit solution.

Palantir stock falls due to planned DoD budget cuts as institutional ownership decreases. AI strategy remains promising.
news.summary: Palantir's stock saw a decline as a result of looming Department of Defense budget reductions. Despite this, supporters are enthusiastic about the tech company's focus on artificial intelligence. The company experienced a drop in institutional ownership, causing its stock to break a crucial technical level. Investors are now debating whether Palantir stock presents a buying opportunity or if it's time to sell.

news.headline: Federal Reserve's key inflation index shows 2.5% growth in Jan 2025, down from 2.6% in Dec, first slowdown in 4 months.
news.summary: On Friday, data from the government revealed that the headline PCE inflation, a key measure watched by the Federal Reserve, saw a slight decrease after four consecutive months of growth. The Personal Consumption Expenditures price index rose by 2.5% in January 2025 compared to the previous year, showing a slowdown from December's 2.6%. This figure aligned with predictions made by economists. Additionally, core PCE, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, saw an increase of 2.6% in the same period. This information comes amidst ongoing monitoring by analysts and investors.

news.headline: Hims & Hers Health's stock drops after semaglutide setback in weight-loss drugs battle, despite impactful commercial.
news.summary: After releasing an impactful commercial, Hims & Hers Health witnessed a significant decline in its stock prices. This drop comes as their semaglutide business encounters a setback in the increasingly competitive weight-loss drugs market. The situation reflects the tumultuous journey of Hims stock as it navigates the challenges posed by rivals in the weight-loss drug sector.

news.headline: Fed's key inflation gauge, PCE price index, drops, calming S&P 500 amid Trump tariff concerns.
news.summary: The PCE price index, a key measure of inflation used by the Federal Reserve, showed a decrease, offering some optimism. At the same time, the S&P 500 experienced instability due to tariff threats from President Trump. This development was covered in a live broadcast by Investor's Business Daily. This information suggests that there may be some stability in prices, and the stock market is reacting to the uncertain trade environment. This news could impact investors and traders as they monitor these important indicators.

news.headline: China Vice Premier: US investment is welcomed - Xinhua.
Panama considers solutions for shut down Cobre Panama mine, 5% of GDP and 1.5% of global copper production impacted.
news.summary: Panama is making progress towards resolving the dispute over its largest mining asset, the Cobre Panama copper mine. President José Raúl Mulino has hinted at considering innovative solutions to settle the disagreement with First Quantum Minerals (OTCPK: FQVLF). The resolution is pending the completion of social security reforms. Mulino mentioned that discussions are underway at the government level regarding the future of the mine, which had a significant impact on Panama's economy, representing 5% of its GDP and 1.5% of global copper production. The mine was abruptly closed in late 2023. Once the social security issue is addressed, Mulino assured reporters that the government will focus on resolving the situation. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Global Methanol-to-Olefins market valued at $24.5B in 2023, anticipated to reach $44.1B by 2034, growing at 5.5% CAGR. Transitioning to sustainable methanol-based olefin production. China leads in MTO adoption. Major players include CNPC, SABIC, ExxonMobil, Shell, and LyondellBasell Industries.
news.summary: The global Methanol-to-Olefins market was valued at US$24.5 billion in 2023 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 5.5% through 2034, reaching approximately US$44.1 billion by the end of the forecast period. This growth is primarily being driven by the increasing demand for ethylene and propylene in various industries such as plastics, packaging, and synthetic materials. The shift towards methanol-based olefin production is seen as a sustainable and cost-effective solution as industries look to reduce their dependence on crude oil. China is leading the way in adopting Methanol-to-Olefins technology, utilizing its coal-to-methanol production capacity to enhance petrochemical self-sufficiency. The process is also gaining traction globally due to its environmentally friendly nature, utilizing renewable sources like biomass and waste to produce olefins, thereby reducing reliance on fossil fuels and supporting a circular economy. Major players in the market include China National Petroleum Corporation, SABIC, ExxonMobil, Shell Global Solutions, and LyondellBasell Industries. This transformative shift in the Methanol-to-Olefins market is bridging the gap between traditional petrochemical and sustainable chemical industries, offering a non-petroleum route to important chemicals like ethylene and propylene.

news.headline: Paul Goepfert, MD chosen by AIM as principal investigator for study of Ampligen & FluMist for avian influenza vaccine.
news.summary: A study is being proposed to investigate the potential of using Ampligen as a vaccine adjuvant to enhance cross-reactivity against various influenza strains. The study aims to explore how Ampligen can boost the effectiveness of influenza vaccines by increasing their ability to provide immunity against different strains. This research initiative, involving a significant number of participants, seeks to evaluate the efficacy and safety of incorporating Ampligen into influenza vaccines to potentially improve overall protection levels. The study will examine the impact of Ampligen as an adjuvant on enhancing the body's immune response and ability to fight off diverse influenza viruses.

Analyst predicts Bitcoin will reach $500,000 before Trump leaves office due to positive outlook for cryptocurrencies. Restrictions have eased for US financial institutions. Policy changes causing uncertainty.
news.summary: Standard Chartered’s Head of Digital Assets Research, Geoff Kendrick, expressed cautious optimism towards cryptocurrencies amidst recent market volatility. In an interview on CNBC, Kendrick highlighted a positive medium-term outlook for cryptocurrencies during the Trump administration. He emphasized the removal of SAB 121 as a significant event, which has relaxed barriers for U.S. financial institutions to enter the crypto market. Kendrick also pointed out that recent policy changes, especially regarding tariffs, have led to uncertainty for various risk assets, including cryptocurrencies. The evolving initiatives in the past few weeks have created confusion for these assets, shaping the narrative of current market dynamics. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
